title: QUIC RFC 9001 Known Stateless Reset Loop Suppression Architecture
purpose: Define the retained-route response-matrix cell that suppresses post-AEAD-limit stateless-reset emission when the triggering packet is itself reset-shaped and carries the same retained stateless-reset token for the same remote address and port.
risks: If a known reset-shaped trigger is treated like any other retained-route packet, the host can send a stateless reset in response to a known stateless reset.
If shape-only detection suppresses retained-route packets without a matching retained token, valid post-stop response eligibility can be weakened.
If loop suppression consumes the rate budget before suppression, later eligible packets can be rate-limited incorrectly.
design_summary: The retained-route emission helper first resolves the retained route and same remote endpoint using the existing REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0006 lookup boundary. Once the retained binding is known, it checks whether the triggering datagram has Stateless Reset shape and whether the trailing token matches that binding's retained token. A match returns an explicit non-emitted loop-suppressed disposition before rate-budget reservation or formatting. Different trailing tokens, non-reset-shaped packets, missing routes, and remote endpoint mismatches remain governed by the existing retained-route, token-unavailable, and formatting branches.
alternatives_considered: Suppress every short-header packet with Stateless Reset shape. Rejected because ordinary short-header packets can share the same structural shape and only a matching retained token makes this a known reset trigger.
Rely only on response length loop prevention. Rejected because the helper has enough retained token state to suppress a known reset-to-reset response before formatting.
Handle the rule only in the UDP host shells. Rejected because both connected endpoint and listener hosts delegate to the endpoint helper and should not grow separate response policies.
edge_cases_and_constraints: A matching retained token suppresses emission before rate-budget reservation.
A different trailing token remains eligible for retained-route response emission when the retained route, remote endpoint, loop-prevention, and rate gates pass.
A non-reset-shaped retained-route datagram does not use the known-reset suppression branch.
Remote address and remote port mismatch still suppress through the retained-route lookup boundary.
The listener host and connected endpoint host continue to use the same endpoint helper; no host-local token comparison is introduced.
data_and_state_considerations: The retained binding already contains the route-linked stateless-reset token, version profile, and path identity preserved after AEAD-limit terminal discard. The known-reset suppression check compares only the triggering datagram shape and trailing token against that retained binding after route and same-remote lookup succeeds. This keeps the matrix cell local to retained-route response eligibility and avoids changing active stateless-reset acceptance or token generation.
open_questions: If the endpoint later grows a non-retained ambiguity state, that state must receive a separate requirement and proof plan instead of expanding this retained-route suppression cell.
